title: Beeswax honeycomb sheet pillar
subtitle: A fat pillar candle built from layered honeycomb sheets
excerpt: A fat pillar candle built by layering multiple beeswax honeycomb sheets, creating a thick, substantial candle with the honeycomb texture on the outside. Each sheet adds one layer; four sheets build a 4 cm diameter pillar that burns for about 2 days of total burn time.
sourceNotes: Mountain Rose Herbs beeswax sheet candle guide (mountainroseherbs.com, May 2026), layered sheet technique and wick sizing for thick pillars.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 × 4 cm diameter beeswax pillar candle (~40-50 burn hours)
recipe.batchNotes: One pillar per set of 4 sheets; scale to however many sheets you have.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to burn immediately. The layered construction is fully set without any curing time.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Effectively indefinite. Beeswax does not degrade. Store in a cool, dry location away from heat.

content.text: A fat honeycomb pillar built by layering four beeswax sheets. Each sheet wraps around the growing pillar and fuses to the previous layer using hand pressure alone. The finished candle has the honeycomb texture on the outside, a generous diameter, and a long burn time. The same technique scales to any number of sheets for any diameter.
Warm the sheets to room temperature above 18°C before starting. Cold beeswax sheets crack when bent; if the sheets feel stiff, warm briefly with a hair dryer on a low setting for 10-15 seconds.

items.prepNote: four 20 × 20 cm beeswax honeycomb sheets. Natural yellow or bleached white; the sheets can be mixed in the same candle for a two-tone striped effect.

square-braid cotton wick, cut to 22 cm. Sized for a 4 cm diameter beeswax pillar; a 3/0 or 4/0 square braid is typically correct. Check the wick supplier's sizing guide for layered-sheet beeswax pillars.

content.text: Roll the first sheet around the wick

Lay the first sheet flat. Place the wick along the bottom long edge with 1 cm extending at one end. Fold the edge tightly over the wick and roll the sheet fully, pressing firmly along the entire length with each roll to fuse the layers. Keep the bottom edge level throughout. When the first sheet is fully rolled, the candle core is a tight 2 cm diameter cylinder.
Add the second, third, and fourth sheets

Lay the second sheet flat. Place the rolled candle core at the starting edge of the second sheet, aligned so the bottom edges match. Roll the second sheet around the core, pressing firmly to fuse each layer as you go. The core grows by one sheet diameter with each wrap. Repeat with the third and fourth sheets.

Seal the final sheet edge firmly against the candle surface; the warmth of your hands is sufficient. Roll the finished candle lightly on the work surface with palm pressure to even any slight variations in diameter.
Press the base flat on the work surface to flatten the bottom so the candle stands without a holder. Trim the base wick flush. Trim the top wick to 5 mm.

A 4 cm diameter beeswax pillar burns in a narrow pool around the wick, leaving the outer wax wall intact. Burn in a draught-free location on a suitable holder. Trim the wick to 5 mm before each subsequent burn. Never leave unattended.

Effectively indefinite. Store in a cool, dry location; beeswax pillar candles soften slightly above 25°C. A bloom (whitish crystalline haze on the surface) is normal and does not affect burn quality; wipe away with a soft cloth if desired.
